classes.subject: History - India and the Contemporary World - I

possible_chapters: Socialism in Europe and the Russian Revolution
Nazism and the Rise of Hitler
topics: Quest for imperialism and First World War
subtopics: Map pointing: France, Central Powers, Allied Powers (WWI)
Map pointing: Axis Powers, Allied Powers (WWII)

classes.subject: History - India and the Contemporary World - II

possible_chapters: The Rise of Nationalism in Europe
topics: Impact of French Revolution on nation states

possible_chapters: The Making of a Global World
topics: Economic, political, social features pre/post industrialization

possible_chapters: Print Culture and the Modern World
topics: Development of print from East Asia to Europe/India
Raw material availability vs industry location
Impact of roadways and railways on national economy
Need for power sharing in democracy
Challenges in Belgium and Sri Lanka
Comparison of India with other countries
Role of Gender, religion and caste in democracy
Right to vote and duties as citizens
Success factors: quality of government, economic well-being, inequality
Medium of exchange from ancient times
Production across countries, WTO, Fair Globalisation

activities.title: Multiple Assessment Activities
activities.source_text: Quiz, debate, role play, viva-voce, group discussion, visual expression, interactive bulletin boards, gallery walks, exit cards, concept maps, peer assessment, self-assessment etc.

activities.title: Subject Enrichment Activity
activities.source_text: Project work (Interdisciplinary)-Disaster Management / Consumer Rights OR Social Issues OR Sustainable Development

activities.title: Art Integration Activities
activities.source_text: Various forms of art must be integrated in the project work. Use cartoon strips, PPTs, skits, albums, song and dance.

projects.title: Class IX Disaster Management Project
projects.source_text: Every student must undertake one project on Disaster Management. Objectives: Create awareness, prepare to face situations, ensure participation in risk reduction plans.

projects.title: Class IX Interdisciplinary Project
projects.source_text: History Chapter IV Forest Society and Colonialism & Geography Chapter 5 Natural Vegetation and Wildlife. Compare forest situations, evaluate commercial forestry, analyse rebellions.

projects.title: Class X Project Work
projects.source_text: Consumer Awareness OR Social Issues OR Sustainable Development. Objectives: Gain insight, apply concepts, enhance life skills.

projects.title: Class X Interdisciplinary Project
projects.source_text: History Chapter III Making of a Global World & Geography Chapter 7 Lifelines of National Economy & Economics Chapter 4 Globalisation. Analyse implications, discuss globalisation experiences.

marks_distribution: Class IX: Total Marks - 80 (Theory) + 20 (Internal Assessment). Theory includes Map Pointing.
Class X: Total Marks - 80 (Board Exam) + 20 (Internal Assessment).
Question Paper Design Class X: MCQs (1 Mark, 25%), Long Answer Questions (2-3 Marks), Case Study Questions (4 Marks), Map Pointing.
Competency Weightage Class X: Remembering/Understanding (30%), Applying (13.25%), Analysing/Evaluating/Creating (50%).
excluded_or_non_assessed_content: Pastoralists in the Modern World (IX History) - assessed only as part of Periodic Assessment
The Story of Village Palampur (IX Economics) - assessed only as part of Periodic Assessment

Natural Vegetation and Wildlife Map Pointing (IX Geography) - Only map pointing evaluated annually, content not in annual exam marks distribution explicitly separate from other chapters but noted for specific evaluation.
The Age of Industrialisation (X History) - assessed only as part of Periodic Assessment
Lifelines of National Economy Map Pointing (X Geography) - Only map pointing evaluated in Board Examination
teacher_notes: Interdisciplinary projects must be carried out at school between April and September. Carryover to home is strictly avoided.
Projects should use eco-friendly products without excessive expenditure.
Project records (internal assessment) must be maintained for three months after result declaration.
Use of Govt. of India website maps is permitted.
Rubrics for project evaluation: Content accuracy/originality (2), Competencies/Presentation (2), Viva-Voce (1).
Map work includes locating, labeling, and identifying specific areas as listed in the syllabus sections.
